raw_consumer_statement: Purchased 4 tickets ( {$4500.00} ) to the XXXX XXXX XXXX to be held XX/XX/year>. 2 weeks before the event, the promoter ( XXXX XXXX ) cancelled the concert and rescheduled it for XX/XX/XXXX. We couldn't attend and asked for a refund. They would not respond to us. We subsequently asked Citibank ( XXXX card ) to formally dispute the charge. Citibank after a few months of investigation - sided with the promoter and said they had a " no cancel '' clause - even though that clause was for the attendee cancelling ( not the actual service provider ). We tried to escalate - to no avail. Finally, we decided to just stick it out and go to the XXXX concert. Days before the concert, it was cancelled yet again. We never received any tickets- just an email that it was cancelled and refunds will be processed. We have not received anything. We disputed again with the credit card company- and they are refusing to do anything because they say the merchant isn't responsive to them. XXXX Citibank representatives ( and especially the supervisor I spoke with ) were some of the rudest people I've dealt with in the service industry. They understood what we were going through - and, quite frankly, didn't care. I was telling my story- and the supervisor stopped me and said " I appreciate you are feeling better about telling me this story, but it doesn't matter, we aren't going to do anything for you. '' I was speechless!!
